title: Fermented Chewy Irish Dessert Tagine
description: A rich and creamy fermented dessert tagine featuring the sweetness of jam and the chewiness of fermented Irish oats.

ingredients.name: Unflavored Yogurt
ingredients.notes: for the yogurt mash, use a probiotic yogurt for added fermentation

ingredients.name: Jam
ingredients.notes: use a sugar-free jam to maintain the diet's strictness

instructions: Prepare the tagine vessel by washing it with water and drying it with a clean cloth.
Add the Irish oats, apple cider vinegar, and honey to the tagine vessel.
Mix the ingredients together until the oats are well coated with the liquid mixture.
Place the tagine vessel on the stovetop over low heat and let it simmer for 2 hours, or until the oats have absorbed most of the liquid and have a chewy texture.
Stir the mixture occasionally to prevent burning.
After 2 hours, remove the tagine vessel from the heat and let it cool to room temperature.
Meanwhile, prepare the yogurt mash by mixing together the unflavored yogurt, ghee, and coconut water in a separate bowl.
Once the tagine vessel has cooled, add the yogurt mash to the oats and mix until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a serving dish and garnish with fresh rosemary sprigs.
Serve the Fermented Chewy Irish Dessert Tagine chilled, garnished with more rosemary sprigs if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Before serving, let the dessert sit at room temperature for 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Note: This dessert is best served after a few hours or overnight in the refrigerator, as the flavors will meld together and the texture will become even chewier.
Note: If using a probiotic yogurt, the fermentation process will be accelerated, resulting in a tangier and more sour dessert.
Note: If the dessert becomes too sour, add a little honey to balance out the flavors.
